name: USS Abraham Lincoln
area: Gulf of Oman — closer to Hormuz approaches
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye
notes: Primary strike platform for Operation Epic Fury. Shifted closer to Hormuz approaches for mine-clearing support. Air wing flying 200+ sorties/day. Day 12 operations ongoing.

name: USS Gerald R. Ford
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, F-35C Lightning II, EA-18G Growlers
status: Engaged — supporting strikes on Hezbollah targets and Iranian western approaches
notes: Eastern Mediterranean positioning south of Cyprus. Dual mission: Lebanon front support and long-range strikes into western Iran via F-35C.

name: USS George H.W. Bush
area: North Arabian Sea — moving west toward Gulf of Oman
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye
notes: North Arabian Sea off Oman coast. Covering southern Iranian coast, interdicting smuggling routes, providing depth to the carrier screen.

name: USS America
area: Gulf of Oman (near Strait of Hormuz) — mine-clearing ops
aircraft: F-35B Lightning II, MV-22 Osprey, AH-1Z Viper, CH-53E Super Stallion
status: Standing by — mine countermeasures support, TRAP
notes: 11th Marine Expeditionary Unit embarked. F-35Bs flying combat sorties. Marines on standby for personnel recovery and Hormuz mine-clearing support.

name: USS Paul Ignatius
area: Strait of Hormuz approaches — escort operations
notes: Forward-deployed Arleigh Burke-class destroyer. Conducting escort for mine countermeasures vessels in Hormuz approaches. Aegis BMD capable.

name: USS Florida
area: Gulf of Oman (approx.) — repositioned closer to Hormuz
notes: Ohio-class guided missile submarine. Carries up to 154 Tomahawk cruise missiles. Exact position classified — approximate location shown.

name: USS John Finn
notes: Detached from CSG-3 to Red Sea for Houthi missile defense and merchant shipping escort. Key node in southern blockade.
